items.detail: The hidden target identity is only flagged in the speaker prompt; listener prompts expose candidate IDs only as selectable object labels.
items.name: Target ID is never shown to the listener.

items.detail: Integrity checks cover generated/evaluated messages, candidate lists, and ablation audit rows for obj_* leakage.
items.name: Speaker messages are checked for target ID leakage.

items.detail: Every result row has a listener choice that is a valid candidate object ID, and success flags are recomputed from choice_id == target_id.
items.name: Listener outputs are valid object IDs.

items.detail: The integrity audit checks same-scene coverage and balanced rows per scene-method for the paper-facing record files.
items.name: Same test scenes are used across methods.

items.detail: The prompt appendix is generated from the current implementation and records the exact speaker and held-out listener prompts used by the cached artifacts.
items.name: Prompts are frozen before final evaluation.

items.detail: Mirror, population, and consensus+info selection use local training-listener feedback or text priors; held-out API labels are reserved for evaluation and oracle analysis.
items.name: Held-out listeners are not used for method selection.

items.detail: The integrity audit confirms cached response files are present and parse as JSON.
items.name: All responses are cached.

items.detail: The cache audit and paper report exact response model snapshots for both API listener families.
items.name: Exact model versions are reported.

items.detail: The main mixed-results table includes 95% intervals, and paired interval claims are covered by the claim verifier.
items.name: Main table has confidence intervals.

items.detail: The paper localizes the mixed-pilot degradation to perspective-shift scenes and links the scenario table to the artifact guide.
items.name: Scenario table explains where gains/losses occur.

items.detail: The paper defines and reports same-play minus held-out cross-play gaps for the main self-play selectors.
items.name: Cross-play gap is reported.

items.detail: The paper and audits report oracle candidate success to separate generation failures from selector failures.
items.name: Oracle upper bound is reported.

items.detail: The qualitative appendix and paper examples show mirror-selected messages failing held-out listeners while population or consensus+info repairs succeed.
items.name: Manual failure examples support the quantitative result.

items.detail: The introduction frames self-play success as a possible blind spot and asks whether selected messages transfer to held-out listeners.
items.name: Intro states the evaluation blind spot clearly.

items.detail: The readiness audit confirms the Related Work section exists and the bibliography is populated without inflating the workshop draft past the page target.
items.name: Related work is concise.

items.detail: The reproduction file, protocol appendix, and integrity audit expose the command path and protocol surface for the paper-facing artifacts.
items.name: Method is reproducible.

items.detail: The limitations section explicitly narrows the claims to small diagnostic API-listener experiments.
items.name: Limitations are honest.

items.detail: The claim verifier recomputes numeric and required text claims from checked-in result files.
items.name: Claims match actual results.

items.detail: The generated protocol appendix includes prompt text, listener styles, and result/candidate schemas.
items.name: Appendix includes prompts and schemas.
